item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced. Thus, it is to be assumed as non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: Re: Inefficient use of test subjects, perhaps?
item.content:encoded: I have less of a problem with the use of D class, but I absolutely agree that the subject stops dollifying when blood is removed line is odd. It contradicts the rest of the study of the scp entirely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favorite part is the drying to death part because it means your body would look like a ragdoll. I know that's the idea but I love how it doesn't outright state that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Autopsies of the brain motor control centre reveal [DATA EXPUNGED]. "

I feel that this is really unnecessary. After telling us that this thing compels you to kill yourself in a rather gruesome manner I doubt there is much that could be going on in the brain mechanically which anyone would want to keep secret. It feels more like you couldn't be bothered to write anything but still felt like it ought to be included because… I have no idea.
This is a nitpick in an overall decent article (at least for a thing which makes you kill yourself), but I really dislike the use of [DATA EXPUNGED] for the sake of it.

item.title: Inefficient use of test subjects, perhaps?
item.content:encoded:
I think the article itself is done well, but I felt that the researchers involved were excessively liberal in their terminations. If it is obvious that the victims are still under the effect of the SCP, then they could still be useful.
They specifically noticed that the D-class dragged himself along to follow the agent for 15 minutes with one arm? Well, there's probably something up with that, and the scientists killed him to no fault of the D-class. If they were feeling cruel, they could still have found a passable use for him. I might wonder if the subject would continue until all the calories in their body are spent, if they ever pass out, or something similar.
Also, which significant test is the Description referring to that details how the victim stops trying to dollify himself when the blood is cleaned off of the needle?

And, killing an insane helpless test subject is just evil. He could be a test subject for a more medicinal/mind-affecting SCP or something (perhaps held just in case they find one if they don't have one they need to test… maybe you could use him in a future SCP you write?) at the very least if they don't feel like healing him with something that works.
That's what I think, anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, let me guess. The thing actually traps and collects the souls/consciousnesses/whathaveyou in their own dead bodies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There goes any hope of becoming a doctor. Fucking fuckberries, that was creepy.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dear God. Creepiest SCP I've read yet (and I've been reading them in numerical order).

Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You, sir, are sick and twisted, and I like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ♪♫ AARGH, A NOTE TO FOLLOW SEW… ♫♪

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here. That was a nice twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ♪♫ SEW… A NEEDLE PULLING THREAD ♫♪

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: NOPE NOPE NOPE NOPE NOPE NOPE NOPE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"It wanted to keep me."
Made the whole article for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favorite part of this one is "Used to have to do things the old fashioned way." Until that line I was sure that there were no serial killer, just a bunch of accidental deaths caused by 1034 and a guy who was unlucky enough to take the blame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "It wanted to keep me." Aaaaughohgodfuck. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh god it was meh before the picture but oh god that picture

upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I'd have to agree with Anax about the water angle, maybe say it forces you to perspire ██% of moisture from your body.

Other than that, this thing is *horrible*; not the writing, the suture itself. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I like the mystery of how it appeared. That's the strongest part of this, by far. It also answers the question of why this thing seems so mean-spirited. Namely, that it actually is intended to be for a serial killer. Since that makes the article so much stronger, I'd advise putting it at the end. Save the best for last.

Also, removing actually all moisture from someone? You do know that that would mean that they'd be sitting in a massive, massive puddle? And would be more mummified than most mummies? Seriously, removing all water from someone would probably cause complete structural collapse of their bodies. Just have it desiccate them. Removing all water is just silly if you think through what that would do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. I'll be hiding under my blanket now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't feel they exhausted questioning of D-3826 before subjecting him to experimentation, but I'll up vote - and since I'm running a fever I bet I have awful dreams AND IT'S ALL YOUR FAULT!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll upvote it just as long as you keep it away from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of like it. A serial killing needle and thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am 100% meh on this one.
